title: K8s Pod Details & Scheduling
description: Rich pod lifecycle: phases, start/completion/deletion times, exit reasons, pending analysis, scheduling delay, readiness, restarts.

panels.title: Running Pods
panels.description: Current count of pods in Running phase

panels.title: Pending Pods
panels.description: Current count of pods in Pending phase

panels.title: Succeeded Pods
panels.description: Current count of pods in Succeeded phase

panels.title: Failed Pods
panels.description: Current count of pods in Failed phase

panels.title: Unknown Pods
panels.description: Current count of pods in Unknown phase

panels.title: Total Pods
panels.description: Total pod count across all phases

panels.title: Pods by Phase Over Time
panels.description: Single query split by phase label. Each color = a phase.

panels.title: Full Pod Lifecycle — Start, End, Exit Reason
All Pods — Full Lifecycle Table
panels.description:
Every pod with complete lifecycle timestamps:
• Started At = kube_pod_start_time (when first container started)
• Completed At = kube_pod_completion_time (when pod finished, for Jobs/completed pods)
• Deleted At = kube_pod_deletion_timestamp (when pod deletion was requested)
• Exit Reason = kube_pod_container_status_last_terminated_reason (OOMKilled, Error, Completed, etc.)
• Uptime = how long since pod started
• Total Restarts = cumulative container restart count

If Completed/Deleted columns are empty, the pod is still active.

panels.title: Container Exit Reasons — Last Termination per Container
panels.description:
For every container, the reason it was last terminated:
• OOMKilled = container exceeded memory limit, killed by K8s
• Error = container exited with non-zero exit code (application crash)
• Completed = container finished normally (exit code 0)
• ContainerCannotRun = container runtime error
• DeadlineExceeded = Job exceeded activeDeadlineSeconds
• Evicted = pod evicted by kubelet (disk/memory pressure)

This shows the most recent termination — useful for diagnosing crash loops.

panels.title: Containers Stuck in Waiting State (by Reason)
panels.description:
Containers NOT running — stuck waiting.
• CrashLoopBackOff = keeps crashing, K8s backing off restarts
• ImagePullBackOff / ErrImagePull = cannot pull image
• CreateContainerConfigError = missing secret/configmap
• ContainerCreating = still starting up (normal if brief)

panels.title: Containers Killed or Terminated (by Reason)
panels.description:
Containers that were forcefully stopped.
• OOMKilled = ran out of memory
• Error = exited non-zero (app crash)
• Completed = finished normally
• ContainerCannotRun = runtime error

panels.title: Which Pods Are Stuck Right Now
panels.description: Pods with containers in Waiting state. These are NOT running.

panels.title: Pod Status Reasons (Evicted, NodeLost, etc.)
panels.description:
• Evicted = kubelet evicted the pod (disk/memory/PID pressure)
• NodeLost = node became unreachable
• UnexpectedAdmissionError = admission webhook error

panels.title: Pod Readiness — Ready vs Not Ready
panels.description: Ready = passing readiness probe, receiving traffic. Not Ready = exists but NOT receiving traffic.

panels.title: Container Restart Rate (restarts/sec)
panels.description: Rate of restarts per pod. Any sustained rate > 0 = pod is crash-looping.

panels.title: Pending Pod Analysis — How Long Pods Wait Before Running
Pending Pods Over Time
panels.description: How many pods are in Pending state at any point. A rising trend = cluster cannot schedule pods fast enough (resource pressure, taints, node selectors, PVC issues).

panels.title: Individual Pods in Pending State
panels.description: Each line = a pod currently in Pending. If a line persists for minutes, the pod cannot be scheduled. Check events for the reason.

panels.title: Currently Pending Pods — How Long They Have Been Waiting
panels.description:
Pods currently in Pending phase. Shows:
• Created At = when the pod spec was submitted to the API (kube_pod_created * 1000)
• Pending Duration = time() - kube_pod_created (how long it's been waiting)

If Created At shows empty or 1970, the kube_pod_created metric may not be available in your kube-state-metrics version. In that case, use 'kubectl get pod -o wide' to check creation times.

Common reasons for being stuck in Pending:
- Insufficient CPU/Memory/GPU on any node
- Node taints with no matching tolerations
- Node selector / affinity rules no node matches
- PVC not bound (storage not available)
- Too many pods on node (PodLimit)

panels.title: Pods: Scheduled vs Not Scheduled
panels.description:
kube_pod_status_scheduled condition=true: scheduler found a node.
condition=false: no suitable node (insufficient resources, taints).
All 'Not Scheduled' pods are a subset of Pending pods.

panels.title: Unscheduled Pods (scheduler cannot find a node)
panels.description:
These pods have PodScheduled=False. The scheduler tried and failed.
Run: kubectl describe pod <name> | grep Events
to see the exact reason (e.g. 'Insufficient nvidia.com/gpu').

panels.title: Historical Scheduling Delay (for pods that did start)
Time from Creation to Running (per pod)
panels.description:
kube_pod_start_time - kube_pod_created = total delay from API submission to first container running. Includes: scheduling + image pull + init containers.

NOTE: Requires kube_pod_created metric (kube-state-metrics v1.6+). If this shows 'No data', your kube-state-metrics may not expose kube_pod_created.

panels.title: Pod Start Times (when pods went Running)
panels.description: Shows when each pod transitioned to Running. Useful for correlating with cluster events (scaling, deployments, node additions).

panels.title: Pods by Startup Delay (longest first)
panels.description:
How long each pod took from creation to first container start. Sorted by longest delay.

NOTE: Requires kube_pod_created metric. If empty, this metric is not available in your cluster.
